resume_text: CS graduate with strong fundamentals in Python and REST API design. Built a personal expense-tracking API using FastAPI, PostgreSQL, and Docker during a 3-month self-directed project. Completed two open-source contributions — a bug fix for a popular Flask plugin and a test suite for a Django utility. Understands HTTP, JWT authentication, basic indexing, and CI pipelines. Comfortable with Git workflows including rebasing and PR reviews.
project_signals: Built full personal expense-tracking API with auth, CRUD, and Dockerised deployment
2 accepted open-source contributions to popular Python web libraries
CI pipeline with automated test gate on every PR
impact_signals: Open-source bug fix resolved an issue affecting 200+ downstream users
Personal project API handles 100 req/s on a single t2.micro
ownership_signals: Sole architect and developer of personal project from design to deployment
Authored API documentation and OpenAPI schema without prompting

resume_text: 2.5 years as a backend engineer at a B2C travel-tech startup. Owns the booking microservice handling 15K daily transactions. Migrated legacy monolith endpoints to FastAPI with async SQLAlchemy, reducing average response time from 420ms to 95ms. Introduced structured logging and distributed tracing with OpenTelemetry. Writes thorough integration tests with testcontainers. Mentors two interns. On-call rotation participant.
project_signals: Owns booking microservice — 15K daily transactions in production
Led monolith-to-microservice migration for 4 legacy endpoints
impact_signals: Average API response time reduced from 420ms to 95ms post-migration
On-call P1 incident rate dropped 60% after structured logging rollout
Test coverage on booking service increased from 38% to 87%
ownership_signals: Sole owner of booking microservice design, code, and runbook
Ran migration planning and rollout without senior oversight
Authored and presented architecture decision records (ADRs) for 3 design choices

resume_text: 6 years backend engineering across fintech and edtech. Designed and delivered a multi-tenant API gateway serving 2M monthly active users. Led a database sharding initiative that scaled peak throughput from 3K to 22K req/s. Contributed to public RFC for async task queue design. Conducts system design interviews for mid-level hiring. Proficient in Python, Go, and TypeScript. Strong opinions on observability, incident response, and technical debt prioritisation.
project_signals: Designed multi-tenant API gateway — 2M MAU in production
Led DB sharding initiative across 3 PostgreSQL clusters
Delivered async task queue RFC adopted by 2 internal teams
impact_signals: Peak throughput scaled from 3K to 22K req/s after sharding
P99 latency held below 150ms under 10× traffic spike
0 data-loss incidents across 6 years of on-call tenure
ownership_signals: Accountable for API gateway SLA, runbooks, and capacity planning
Defined team-wide coding standards and PR review process
Runs system design interviews — has assessed 40+ candidates

resume_text: Former data engineer (3 years, Python + Spark + Airflow) who transitioned to backend API development 18 months ago. Strong SQL and data-pipeline intuition translates well to query optimisation and ETL-adjacent microservices. Rebuilt a reporting API that replaced nightly batch jobs with real-time aggregations, cutting report generation from 8 hours to under 2 minutes. Learning FastAPI and async patterns; background in robust error handling and large-dataset edge cases.
project_signals: Rebuilt reporting API replacing nightly batch jobs with real-time aggregations
Migrated 3 Airflow DAGs to event-driven microservices
Optimised 12 slow-query endpoints — average query time down 70%
impact_signals: Report generation reduced from 8 hours to under 2 minutes
Query optimisation freed 40% of DB CPU headroom
ownership_signals: Authored migration runbook and trained two data engineers on API patterns
